title: The $1M Retirement Goal Is $1.5M Short
hook: Everyone's chasing a million to retire — but at 4% withdrawal, that's forty grand a year. The average retiree needs $57,818 — and your million runs out in seventeen years.
angle: The iconic $1M retirement target is mathematically broken: it ignores average spending rates, a $315K healthcare bill, and inflation eroding 40% of purchasing power over 20 years — the real number is $2.5M
audience: Working adults who think $1M is the retirement finish line but feel like they're falling behind
key_numbers: 40% of purchasing power lost to inflation in 20 years
$1,600/month to hit $2.5M starting at 30 vs $2,900/month starting at 40
